anchored_threads.thread_name: Karma Yoga / disciplined action — engineering: shipping discipline, deprecation discipline
anchored_threads.anchor_evidence: The verse anchors on the deprecation-discipline sub-thread: misplaced loyalty as a specific failure mode that prevents the operator from completing prescribed work. Tags: decision-paralysis, deprecation-management, team-culture.

progression_issue.explanation: BG 2.4 is positioned correctly — Arjuna's first specific reply after Krishna's diagnosis (2.2) and imperative (2.3). The engineering layer does not pre-empt the chapter's later teachings (the chapter has not yet introduced the atman-survives-change argument that will eventually dissolve this objection at 2.20-2.25). The verse explicitly states 'the chapter will dissolve this by reframing the operative unit; the verse itself names the trap precisely' — pedagogically aware.

verdict_reason: The verse anchors on a specific deprecation-discipline failure mode (misplaced loyalty as paralysis-mechanism). It preserves the chapter's hard edge that duty-under-difficulty is not optional even when difficult — the deprecation must happen, the verse only names why it is being avoided. It does not soften the chapter's voice; the framing analysis remains corrective. The counter-anti-thesis is intact: the verse does not teach disrespect for elders/architects; it teaches the distinction between right-loyalty (collaborative) and wrong-loyalty (deferral).
